fieldGroups.description: Diagnosis evidence, coding-sensitive rows, and escalation flags.

fieldGroups.description: Past medical history, caregiver support, living situation, immunizations, and supplementals.

fieldGroups.description: Identity, agency, physician, and demographic reference values that should not dominate QA.

DOB/Age/Gender: 2/1/1939 87 years Female Attending Phys: Bal D,Harjinder Singh History and Physical "Given the patient's rehabilitation need and on going medical issues, this patient is best served in a comprehensive inpatient rehabilitation setting with an interdisciplinary approach with at least 3 hours of skilled therapies for at least 5 days per week.
Considering all the information above, it is my best judgement that this patient requires intensive therapy in an inpatient rehabilitation hospital due to rehabilitation needs.
This patient can reasonably be expected to participate in and benefit in an interdisciplinary delivery of care under the direction of a rehabilitation or equally certified physician.
BENNETT, SHUMEI

Sumayo noted and appreciated Continue regular home medication Pain control Bowel care Sleep aid if needed Will follow patient closely and make further recommendation as warranted Discussed with the patient daughter at the bedside

Patient lives alone and recently sustained a ground level fall leading to hospitalization where the workup found to have right periorbital ecchymosis/hematoma, nontraumatic rhabdomyolysis, non ST-elevation MI secondary to fall and rhabdomyolysis.
Code status: dnr
Neurologic: global weakness, no focal motor deficit
